title: PLS algorithm for Categorical and Scalar Functional Data
text: Smooth PLS: Integration categorical states indicator functions smoother regression curves. Hybrid Data: Seamlessly handles Scalar Functional Data (SFD) Categorical Functional Data (CFD). Interpretability: Provides continuous regression curves. Comparison Suite: Built-functions compare results Naive (discretized) PLS Standard Functional PLS.

title: PLS algorithm for Categorical and Scalar Functional Data
text: SmoothPLS builds upon fundamental principles Functional PLS (FPLS) regression, specifically approximation functional predictors basis expansions, established Aguilera, Preda et al. (2010) [1]. primary contribution lies modeling categorical state changes functional indicator functions, 𝟙ts\mathbb{1}^s_t. Instead discretizing transitions, model computes components via Active Area Integration (illustrated package logo), effectively integrating basis functions specific intervals, τs\tau_s, state active: Λs,j=∫𝒯𝟙tsϕj(t)dt=∫τsϕj(t)dt,withτs={t∈𝒯∣X(t)=s}𝟙st={1if X(t)=s0otherwise\Lambda_{s,j} = \int_{\mathcal{T}} \mathbb{1}^s_t \phi_j(t) dt = \int_{\tau_s} \phi_j(t) dt, \quad \text{} \quad \tau_s = \lbrace t \\mathcal{T} \mid X(t) = s\rbrace \quad \text{} \quad \mathbb{1}_s^t = \begin{cases} 1 & \text{} X(t) = s \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases} formulation ensures smoothing process respects continuous nature state transitions within Functional PLS framework.

code: library(SmoothPLS)  # 1. Generate Synthetic Data df_x <- generate_X_df(nind = 100, curve_type = 'cat') Y_df <- generate_Y_df(df_x, curve_type = 'cat',                        beta_real_func_or_list = beta_1_real_func)  # 2. Fit Smooth PLS Model basis <- create_bspline_basis(start = 0, end = 100, nbasis = 10) spls_model <- smoothPLS(df_list = df_x, Y = Y_df$Y_noised,                          basis_obj = basis, curve_type_obj = 'cat')  # 3. Predict and Visualize preds <- smoothPLS_predict(df_x, spls_model$reg_obj, curve_type = 'cat') plot(spls_model$reg_obj$CatFD_1_state_1, main="SmoothPLS Regression Curve")

title: PLS algorithm for Categorical and Scalar Functional Data
text: parallel = TRUE, SmoothPLS utilizes future framework parallelize numerical integration steps (e.g., Λ\Lambda matrix evaluation). mitigate computational overhead smaller datasets, package implements dynamic load balancing. calculates optimal number background workers required specific task maximize efficiency. default threshold set 2500 integral evaluations per core. engine allocates one core every 2500 integrals (calculated individuals ×\times basis functions). instance: * 2,500 integrals: model executes sequentially (1 core) avoid setup overhead. * 5,000 integrals: engine allocates exactly 2 cores. * Large datasets (e.g., 50,000+ integrals): engine recruits maximum number available cores, reserving 2 cores maintain operating system stability. threshold can manually adjusted based specific hardware capabilities (e.g., lowered UNIX systems low forking overhead) setting global option model execution:
code: # Lower the threshold to 500 evaluations per core options(SmoothPLS.parallel_threshold = 500)

code: N_states = 3 lambdas = lambda_determination(N_states) transition_df = transfer_probabilities(N_states)  df = generate_X_df_multistates(nind = 100, N_states, start=0, end=100, lambdas,  transition_df) df_processed = cat_data_to_indicator(df)  df_regul = list() df_wide = list() for(name in names(df_processed)){ print(paste0(name, " regularisation")) df_regul[[name]] = regularize_time_series(df_processed[[name]], time_seq =  c(0:100), curve_type = 'cat', id_col='id', time_col='time')  df_wide[[name]] = convert_to_wide_format(df_regul[[name]], id_col='id', time_col='time') } #> [1] "state_1 regularisation" #> [1] "state_2 regularisation" #> [1] "state_3 regularisation"  basis = create_bspline_basis(0, 100, 10, 4)  df_fd = list() for(name in names(df_wide)){ print(paste0(name, " fd transformation")) df_fd[[name]] = fda::Data2fd(argvals = c(0:100), y = t(df_wide[[name]][, -c(1)]), basis) } #> [1] "state_1 fd transformation" #> [1] "state_2 fd transformation" #> [1] "state_3 fd transformation"  plot_real_and_smoothed_data_ind(df_wide, df_fd, c(0:100), id=1) #> [1] "Real and fd values for the individual n=1"

title: SmoothPLS OLD README

title: SmoothPLS OLD README
text: Smooth PLS: Integration categorical states indicator functions smoother regression curves. Hybrid Data: Seamlessly handle Scalar Functional Data (SFD) Categorical Functional Data (CFD). Interpretability: Provides regression curves β\beta even discrete state changes. Comparison Suite: Built-functions compare results Naive (discretized) PLS Standard Functional PLS.

title: SmoothPLS OLD README
text: core innovation lies treating categorical predictors simple dummy variables, functional indicator functions 𝟙ts\mathbb{1}^s_t. 𝟙st={1if X(t)=s0otherwise \mathbb{1}_s^t = \begin{cases} 1 & \text{} X(t) = s \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases} model computes components integrating specific intervals state present: Λs,j=∫𝒯𝟙tsϕj(t)dt=∫τsϕj(t)dt,withτk={t∈𝒯,X(t)=k}\Lambda_{s,j} = \int_{\mathcal{T}} \mathbb{1}^s_t \phi_j(t) dt = \int_{\tau_s} \phi_j(t) dt, \quad \text{} \quad \tau_k = \lbrace t \\mathcal{T}, X(t) = k\rbrace ensures smoothing process respects physical reality state transitions maintaining continuous framework Functional PLS.

title: SmoothPLS OLD README
text: SmoothPLS builds upon fundamental principles Functional PLS (FPLS) regression, specifically approximation functional predictors basis expansions, established Aguilera, Preda et al. (2010) [1]. novel contribution handle categorical state changes functional indicator functions 𝟙ts\mathbb{1}^s_t. Rather discretizing transitions, model computes components Active Area Integration (defined illustrated logo), effectively integrating basis functions specific intervals τs\tau_s state present: Λs,j=∫𝒯𝟙tsϕj(t)dt=∫τsϕj(t)dt,withτk={t∈𝒯,X(t)=k}𝟙st={1if X(t)=s0otherwise\Lambda_{s,j} = \int_{\mathcal{T}} \mathbb{1}^s_t \phi_j(t) dt = \int_{\tau_s} \phi_j(t) dt, \quad \text{} \quad \tau_k = \lbrace t \\mathcal{T}, X(t) = k\rbrace \quad \text{} \quad \mathbb{1}_s^t = \begin{cases} 1 & \text{} X(t) = s \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases} ensures method respects physical reality state transitions maintaining continuous framework Functional PLS.

title: SmoothPLS OLD README
text: Based single-state CFD vignette, fit compare models:
code: library(SmoothPLS)  # 1. Generate Synthetic Data df_x <- generate_X_df(nind = 100, curve_type = 'cat') Y_df <- generate_Y_df(df_x, curve_type = 'cat',                        beta_real_func_or_list = beta_1_real_func)  # 2. Fit Smooth PLS Model basis <- create_bspline_basis(start = 0, end = 100, nbasis = 10) spls_model <- smoothPLS(df_list = df_x, Y = Y_df$Y_noised,                          basis_obj = basis, curve_type_obj = 'cat')  # 3. Predict and Visualize preds <- smoothPLS_predict(df_x, spls_model$reg_obj, curve_type = 'cat') plot(spls_model$reg_obj$CatFD_1_state_1, main="SmoothPLS Regression Curve")

title: SmoothPLS OLD README
text: default, SmoothPLS uses future framework automatically parallelize heavy numerical integration steps (Lambda matrix evaluation) parallel = TRUE. prevent performance degradation (overhead) smaller datasets, package uses dynamic load balancing. Instead simple binary /switch, calculates optimal number background workers required specific task maximize efficiency. default threshold set 2500 integral evaluations per core. engine recruit one core every 2500 integrals (calculated Individuals ×\timesBasis functions). example: * 2,500 integrals: model runs sequentially (1 core) avoid unnecessary setup overhead. * 5,000 integrals: engine spins exactly 2 cores. * Massive datasets (e.g., 50,000+ integrals): engine recruits maximum number available cores machine (always leaving 2 cores free keep operating system responsive). can manually adjust threshold based specific hardware (e.g., lower UNIX system low forking overhead) setting global option running model:
code: # Lower the threshold to 500 evaluations per core options(SmoothPLS.parallel_threshold = 500)

title: SmoothPLS 0.1.4 (2026-04-09)
text: Implemented dynamic load balancing parallel computing evaluate_lambda functions. Numerical integration steps now 8x faster multi-core machines preventing RAM overhead massive datasets (e.g. >100.000 evaluations).

title: SmoothPLS 0.1.4 (2026-04-09)
text: Launched official pkgdown website (hosted GitHub Pages) including detailed vignettes function references. Added complete compiled PDF manual inst/doc/. Added comprehensive quick-start example (One-State Categorical PLS) visual outputs. Fixed LaTeX equations rendering cross-compatibility GitHub Pandoc/pkgdown. Added institutional links (Decathlon SportsLab, Inria). Configured GitHub Actions workflows automated R CMD check pkgdown site deployment.

title: SmoothPLS 0.1.4 (2026-04-09)
text: Numerical Precision: Optimized categorical integration evaluate_id_func_integral stricter relative tolerance (rel.tol) increased subdivisions (1000) high-order B-splines. Analytic Prediction: Implemented analytic L2 inner product Scalar Functional Data (SFD) using fda::inprod, replacing discrete trapezoidal integration near-perfect precision. Safety Checks: Added time-range assertions smoothPLS_predict prevent silent errors predicting data outside basis domain.

title: SmoothPLS 0.1.4 (2026-04-09)
text: Tidyselect Compatibility: Fixed deprecation warnings implementing all_of() data pivoting functions. Multivariate Support: Corrected logical assertions smoothPLS properly handle mixed lists categorical numerical predictors. Integration Robustness: Added stop..error = FALSE segment integration handle micro-intervals without crashing full model.

title: SmoothPLS 0.1.4 (2026-04-09)
text: Core Test Suite: Added 70 unit tests covering Theorems (univariate equivalence), score orthogonality, prediction consistency. Edge Cases: Added tests time-mismatch handling multi-state categorical transitions.

title: SmoothPLS 0.1.4 (2026-04-09)
text: Code Refactoring: Modularization internal functions Lambda matrix evaluation. Synthetic Data: Improved generate_X_df generate_Y_df realistic categorical state transitions. S3 Structure Prep: Initial work internal objects support future S3 methods (print, plot, predict).
